records.display_name: Farebox Revenue Favorable to Forecast Drivers

payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenues were $5.3 million favorable to the Forecast due to increased non-commutation ridership and higher average yield per passenger partially offset by lower commutation ridership.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

records.raw_text: Farebox Revenues were $5.3 million favorable to the Forecast due to increased non-commutation ridership and higher average yield per passenger partially offset by lower commutation ridership.

records.display_name: Non-Labor Expenses $6.1M Favorable to Forecast

payload.claim_text: Non-Labor Expenses: $6.1 million favorable to the Forecast.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

records.display_name: Total Expenses $7.1M Favorable to Forecast

payload.claim_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,268.0 million were $7.1 million or 0.6% favorable to the Forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance include lower pensions and lower health and welfare costs.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

records.raw_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,268.0 million were $7.1 million or 0.6% favorable to the Forecast.

records.display_name: Non-Reimbursable Operating Results $10.2M Favorable

payload.claim_text: August YTD non-reimbursable operating results were favorable to the Forecast by $10.2 million or 1.2%. Non-reimbursable revenues through August were $12.0 million favorable to the Forecast, primarily due to higher advertising, interest, non-commutation ridership and average yield per passenger. Total non-reimbursable expenses were $6.3 million unfavorable primarily due to higher other fringe benefits and payroll expenses.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

records.raw_text: August YTD non-reimbursable operating results were favorable to the Forecast by $10.2 million or 1.2%.

records.display_name: Labor Expenses $12.4M Unfavorable to Forecast

payload.claim_text: Labor Expenses: $12.4 million unfavorable to the Forecast. Payroll was $4.5 million unfavorable due to reduced capital project activity, higher GASB 101 accruals, and vacation buyback expense partially offset by lower OTE Retro-Wage accruals and decreased hiring activity.
